sections.instruction: Questions 1-6: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN ONE WORD OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 7-10: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Hostel Accommodation in Darwin
Kangaroo lodge
General information about hostel accommodation

sections.instruction: Questions 11-16: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D. Questions 17-20: Label the map below. Write the correct letter A-F next to questions 17-20.

questions.content: The land where the Sculpture Park is located was previously
options: A completely covered by forest.
B the site of a private house.
C occupied by a factory.

questions.content: What is unusual about the Anglia Sculpture Park?
options: A Artists have made sculptures especially for it.
B Some of its sculptures were donated by the artists.
C It only shows contemporary sculptures.

questions.content: What is the theme of Joe Tremain’s ‘burnt’ sculptures?
options: A the contrast between nature and urban life
B the effect of man on the environment
C the violence of nature

questions.content: The path by the Lower Lake
options: A is rather wet in some places.
B has recently been repaired.
C is difficult to walk on.

questions.content: What does the speaker say about the Visitor Centre?
options: A It is being enlarged at present.
B It has received an international award.
C It was designed by a Canadian architect.

questions.content: Today, visitors can buy snacks and sandwiches
options: A at the kiosk.
B in the Terrace Room.
C at the Lower Lake Cafe.
A
B
C
D
E
F

sections.instruction: Questions 21-26: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 27-30: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content: Why did Leo choose instant coffee as the topic for his marketing report?
options: A He found plenty of material on the topic.
B He had some practical experience in the area.
C He had an idea of a brand he wanted to target.

questions.content: Leo discovered that in Australia, recent technological developments
options: A are producing less healthy types of instant coffee.
B are reducing the demand for instant coffee.
C are improving the quality of instant coffee.

questions.content: What do the speakers agree about Leo’s table of coffee products?
options: A It needs more explanation in the text.
B It is factually inaccurate in some places.
C It would be best to put this in the appendix.

questions.content: What do they decide about the description of Shaffers coffee as a market follower?
options: A Leo needs to define his terms.
B Leo needs to provide more evidence.
C Leo needs to put it in a different section.

questions.content: What does Anna say about originality in someone’s first marketing report?
options: A Clear analysis of data can be considered original.
B Graphs and diagrams should be original, not copied.
C Reports should contain some original data collected by the student.

questions.content: What difference between his school assignments and this report has surprised Leo?
options: A not knowing the criteria for getting a good mark
B being required to produce work without assistance
C having to do a great deal of research

questions.content:
Notes on specific sections of marketing report
Executive summary
• Give a brief overview including the (27) {{BLANK}}
Problems
• Link each problem to a (28) {{BLANK}} which explains it
Implementation
• Practical solutions to problems
• Include details such as participants, (29) {{BLANK}} and sequence
• Section is often poorly done because of lack of (30) {{BLANK}}
Conclusion
• Don’t use new material here

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content:
History of fireworks in Europe
13th-16th centuries
• Fireworks were introduced from China
• Their use was mainly to do with:
o War
o (31) {{BLANK}} (in plays and festivals)
17th century
• Various features of (32) {{BLANK}} were shown in fireworks displays
• Scientists were interested in using ideas from fireworks displays:
o To make human (33) {{BLANK}} possible
o To show the formation of (34) {{BLANK}}
• London:
o Scientists were distrustful at first
o Later they investigated (35) {{BLANK}} uses of fireworks
• St Petersburg:
o Fireworks were seen as a work of (36) {{BLANK}} for people
• Paris:
o Displays emphasized the power of the (37) {{BLANK}}
o Scientists aimed to provide (38) {{BLANK}}
18th century
• Italian fireworks specialists became influential
• Servandoni’s fireworks display followed the same pattern as an (39) {{BLANK}}
• The appeal of fireworks extended to the middle classes
• Some displays demonstrated new scientific discoveries such as (40) {{BLANK}}
